introSection:
Wodan Timbur coaster is a wooden roller coaster located at Europa-Park in Rust, Baden Wuerttemberg, Germany. It opened on 31 March 2012 and was built by Great Coasters International.

The ride is located in the Iceland section of the park, which opened in 2009. It is the park's first and only wooden coaster. Plus, the attraction features a restroom halfway throughout the queue line.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Titan Track was added to the brake run by Great Coasters International and Skyline Attractions in 2023 or earlier. A noise barrier was also added to reduce noise complaints from residents.

Additional Titan Track was added before the lift hill in 2025.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: The coaster was named after the Germanic name of the Nordic god Odin. Timbur is the Icelandic word for wood.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The coaster intertwines with two attractions, which are Atlantica SuperSplash and Blue fire Megacoaster.

sections.title: Theme
sections.content: Wodan Timbur Coaster is themed to Norse mythology. The queue line features several heavily detailed elements, including fire, mist and water effects. The goddess of death, Hel, is also featured and has a turning sand timer. Projection Mapping, smoke screens and moving ceilings are also used in the indoor sections, which immerse guests in mythology. In the station, statues watch the train leave and turn to watch the train arrive. The ride also features tunnels and a station fly-through.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains are GCI Millennium Flyer trains.

fullText:
Wodan Timbur coaster is a wooden roller coaster located at Europa-Park in Rust, Baden Wuerttemberg, Germany. It opened on 31 March 2012 and was built by Great Coasters International.

The ride is located in the Iceland section of the park, which opened in 2009. It is the park's first and only wooden coaster. Plus, the attraction features a restroom halfway throughout the queue line.

Titan Track was added to the brake run by Great Coasters International and Skyline Attractions in 2023 or earlier. A noise barrier was also added to reduce noise complaints from residents.

Additional Titan Track was added before the lift hill in 2025.

The coaster was named after the Germanic name of the Nordic god Odin. Timbur is the Icelandic word for wood.

The coaster intertwines with two attractions, which are Atlantica SuperSplash and Blue fire Megacoaster.

Wodan Timbur Coaster is themed to Norse mythology. The queue line features several heavily detailed elements, including fire, mist and water effects. The goddess of death, Hel, is also featured and has a turning sand timer. Projection Mapping, smoke screens and moving ceilings are also used in the indoor sections, which immerse guests in mythology. In the station, statues watch the train leave and turn to watch the train arrive. The ride also features tunnels and a station fly-through.

The trains are GCI Millennium Flyer trains.
